children.title: Self-hosting decoder encoded as its own image
children.description: Encode the pixelpack decoder itself as a pixelpack multi-pixel PNG. The decoder contains its own blueprint -- a 4x4 or 8x8 image that, when booted, produces the Python decoder that can boot any other image.
enables: Bootstrapping from nothing -- send someone one image and they can decode ALL images
Recursive self-reference opens meta-strategies where programs can modify the expansion context itself
Proof that the system is complete -- if it can encode its own interpreter, it can encode anything
children.risk: Constrains future decoder growth -- any change must still be self-encodable, potentially limiting strategy complexity

children.title: WASM decoder under 512 bytes
children.description: Build a pixelpack decoder in hand-optimized WASM that fits in under 512 bytes. Small enough to embed in a tweet, a QR code payload, or inline in any HTML page as a data URI.
enables: QR codes that ARE working programs -- scan and run, no server needed
Inline program distribution in any medium that supports images (slack, discord, text messages)
IoT and embedded scenarios where a microcontroller receives a pixel and expands it into firmware logic
children.risk: WASM instruction set constraints may force simplified strategies, creating a permanent compatibility split between 'full' and 'embedded' decoders

children.title: Evolutionary seed garden with fitness breeding
children.description: A genetic algorithm system where populations of pixels mutate and crossover, evaluated against fitness functions (does the expanded program pass tests? does it match a target output?). Users define what 'good' means and the system breeds toward it.
enables: Discovering useful programs no human would think to write -- the search space is navigated by evolution
Inverse encoding: describe what you WANT (fitness function) and let the system find a pixel that produces it
Interactive 'gardening' metaphor where users crossbreed their favorite programs to produce offspring
children.risk: Non-determinism breaks the core promise -- same pixel should always produce same program, but evolution implies the journey matters more than reproducibility

children.title: Pixel-art-semantic encoding layer
children.description: Design strategies so that useful programs produce visually meaningful pixel arrangements. A drawing tool where the visual appearance of what you paint correlates with what the program does. A house-shaped image generates code about structures.
enables: Programs readable by humans before decoding -- visual pre-comprehension
Steganographic programming -- images that look like art but are functional code
New art form: constrain visual aesthetics to produce functional beauty, like calligraphy constraints producing poetry
children.risk: Massively reduces encoding efficiency -- optimizing for visual semantics fights against information density

children.title: Hash-chained sequential expansion
children.description: Each pixel's expanded output feeds as additional context into the next pixel's expansion. Pixel N+1's strategy can reference what pixel N produced. Creates sequential dependency chains where order matters and tampering breaks downstream code.
enables: Tamper-evident programs -- modifying any pixel invalidates a cryptographic hash of everything after it
Conditional expansion -- later pixels can adapt their output based on what earlier pixels produced, enabling actual branching logic
Content-addressable program fragments that can be shared and verified independently
children.risk: Destroys parallel decoding, makes partial recovery impossible if one pixel is corrupted

children.title: Open strategy protocol with community genome
children.description: Publish a spec for registering new strategies. Let the community propose, implement, and vote on strategy additions via pull requests. The 'genome' becomes a living, evolving shared resource rather than a fixed 16-entry table.
enables: Domain-specific expansion strategies (webdev snippets, SQL patterns, shader operations) contributed by domain experts
Competitive strategy design where people optimize for coverage of real-world program corpora
Forking the genome for specialized applications -- a genome for bioinformatics, a genome for games
children.risk: Fragmentation -- different decoder versions support different strategies, breaking the 'any image decodes anywhere' promise

children.title: Multi-domain expansion beyond programs
children.description: Strategies that expand pixels into non-program targets: MIDI music sequences, SVG vector drawings, GLSL shader code, neural network weight matrices, game level data, electronic circuit netlists. The pixel becomes a universal generative seed, not just a program seed.
enables: A single image containing both a program AND the data it operates on -- music and the player, artwork and the renderer
Cross-domain pollination where strategies from one domain (RLE patterns from programs) become useful in another (musical patterns)
Generative art pipeline where small pixel images produce complex multimedia outputs when booted in different contexts
children.risk: Loss of conceptual clarity -- 'one pixel is a working program' becomes 'one pixel is... something generative', weakening the core identity
